## Conclusion
- The article package should lead with the evidence-bounded thesis that upstream names are the cleanest first-round beneficiaries, while shipping remains conditional and downstream chemicals remain a margin-risk bucket.

## Confirmed
- The source layer is strong enough to support an article package outline because it clearly separates confirmed, unresolved, and inference-only items.
- The strongest supported opening line is that Brent risk premium moved first while no prolonged physical closure is confirmed in the pack.
- `中国海油` is the cleanest direct beneficiary, and that claim can stay attached to explicit citation anchors.

## Unconfirmed
- Shipping rerating persistence and immediate downstream margin squeeze still belong in the unresolved bucket rather than the confident thesis line.
- The image assets support the package, but they do not by themselves resolve the unresolved company-level claims.

## Risks
- A polished article draft could overstate conditional shipping upside if it writes as if persistence were already confirmed.
- Treating chart images as if they prove the full company-transmission story would outrun the evidence layer.

## Next Step
- Convert this package into a brief-plus-draft artifact with citation tags and image references, then run a structured review pass that removes any claim still sitting in the unresolved bucket.
